taskSummary: Fix persistent Project Dashboard preview loading by hardening the Cloudflare Pages API proxy rather than adding x-include fallback chrome.

bundlesSkipped.reason: No OpenAI API, model, retrieval, structured output, eval or AI runtime change was in scope.
No MCP server, MCP client, tool contract or resource exposure change was in scope.
No Airtable schema, formula, record mutation or attachment contract was changed.

diagnosis: The Project Dashboard calls same-origin /api routes from the Pages preview. Those requests are handled by the Pages Function proxy before reaching the preview Worker. The proxy did not have a finite upstream timeout, so a slow or stalled Worker call could leave the preview route apparently loading. x-include was not changed.
changes: Added bounded upstream timeout handling to functions/api/[[path]].js.
Added configurable timeout clamping through API_PROXY_TIMEOUT_MS or RESEARCHOPS_API_PROXY_TIMEOUT_MS.
Added proxy timeout and upstream diagnostic headers.
Added a route-state test guarding proxy timeout behaviour and guarding against x-include fallback chrome.
residualRisks: If the browser still loads after deployment, the pending request should now resolve as a finite timeout or identify a different stuck request.
This change does not prove the preview Worker is healthy; it prevents the Pages Function proxy from hiding a slow upstream call.
A final Cloudflare Pages preview visual check remains required.
